claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Child Benefit is assessed on a per-child basis, so I'll need information about each child you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question: **Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to check eligibility for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child I'd like to check eligibility for. His name is Alex and he's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old.

Since Alex is 18 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), I need to check if he meets the education or training requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** This would include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent)
- Approved vocational training programs

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, A-levels at sixth form college is approved non-advanced education. Now I need to ask a few more questions about Alex's situation:

**Is Alex studying full-time?** (This means at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's studying full-time at the sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all. He's just focused on his studies.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Next:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's just doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to check about your responsibility for Alex:

**Do you live with Alex, or do you contribute to his upkeep?** (Contributing means paying at least the amount of Child Benefit towards his care - things like food, clothes, housing, pocket money, etc.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Next question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another person responsible for him)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check Alex's living situation:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital?
- In care (local authority care)?
- Being fostered?
- Or is he living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just one final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why Alex qualifies:
- Alex is 18 years old and in approved full-time non-advanced education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- He is not working 24 hours or more per week
- He is not receiving benefits in his own right
- He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you at home
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
1. Apply online or by post using form CH2
2. Provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
3. Provide Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate

**Important note:** Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

Do you have any other questions about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great, thank you so much for your help! You've explained everything clearly and I now know that I'm eligible and what I need to do next. I really appreciate it.
